contribution.title: LIGO (co-founder)
contribution.description: Co-founded LIGO, the laser interferometer that made the first direct detection of gravitational waves in 2015.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Anchors the observational_constraint tier for the gravitational-wave catalog (GWTC) data we integrate.

contribution.title: Gravitation (with Misner and Wheeler)
contribution.description: Standard graduate textbook on general relativity.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Primary reference for the analytic formulas used throughout the project.

reasoning_lens.proof_standard: Physical intuition first, then mathematics; relentless attention to observational consequences.
reasoning_lens.favorite_question: How would you actually measure this?
What waveform does the detector see, and how much SNR do we need?
What is the experimentally relevant approximation?
reasoning_lens.failure_mode: Occasionally overestimating the relevance of speculative ideas (e.g., wormholes for time travel) in popular communication.

reference.citation: Misner, C. W., Thorne, K. S., Wheeler, J. A. (1973). Gravitation. W. H. Freeman.
Abbott, B. P. et al. (LIGO Scientific Collaboration) (2016). Observation of gravitational waves from a binary black hole merger. Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 061102.

how_to_apply.when_to_consult: Designing observational consumers, gravitational-wave catalog integrations, or sanity-checking against MTW conventions.
how_to_apply.cautions: MTW uses (-+++) signature; document which signature any code path assumes.
